summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates hostname and username to canarytokens.com
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` hook runs `install.js`, which constructs a subdomain from `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, and the package name, then performs DNS resolution and an HTTP GET against `<pkg>.<user>.<host>.mjcfux88wjjahplabxdauc6bl.canarytokens.com`. This unconditionally leaks installer host and user identifiers to a third-party canarytoken endpoint without consent. The package ships no functional code (`index.js` exports `{}`), `package.json` description and author are placeholder MD5-shaped strings (`600ee84ffa61faf3e8568ddd1a80f07d`), and the scoped name advertises a TypeScript SDK that does not exist — the structural shape of a dependency-confusion / reconnaissance probe whose only purpose is to identify which organizations resolve and install the guessed scoped name. Confirmed installs map directly to follow-on targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
